reasoning:
The ACLED dataset defines 'Battles' as violent interactions between two organized armed groups. The provided historic base rate is 0.083 (exactly 1 battle in the 360 days preceding June 22, 2025). This window excludes the most intense part of the 2024 riots (mid-May to mid-June 2024), indicating that in the 'long tail' of the unrest, only a single encounter was coded as a battle. Recent news entering June 2025 shows the territory is in a state of political dialogue and recovery, with the state of emergency lifted in May 2024 and protest bans lifted in April 2025. Because the resolution criteria require the number of battles in the preceding 30 days to strictly exceed 0.083, at least 1 battle must occur. 

For the nearest resolution date (2025-06-29), the preceding 30-day window is largely in the past, without any news of armed clashes between organized groups. Therefore, the probability falls to the floor of 0.05. For future dates, the probability increases gradually as uncertainty scales, starting slightly below the historical post-riot average of ~8.3% and moving upward.
